questions.question: Explain the Shortest Seek Time First (SSTF) disk scheduling algorithm with an example. Also state its advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: SSTF selects the request closest to current head position
Minimizes seek time compared to FCFS
May cause starvation for distant requests
Example: Given requests at tracks 45, 22, 76, 95, 60 starting at head position 50, the order would be 45, 22, 60, 76, 95

questions.question: Differentiate between SCAN and C-SCAN disk scheduling algorithms in terms of their working mechanism and performance characteristics.
keyPoints: SCAN moves head to one end, servicing requests, then reverses direction
C-SCAN moves to one end, servicing requests, then jumps back to beginning
SCAN provides more uniform wait time; C-SCAN provides better response time
C-SCAN is also called the elevator algorithm

questions.question: Consider a disk queue with pending requests in the following order: 98, 183, 37, 122, 14, 124, 65, 67. The head is initially at track 53 moving towards higher track numbers. Calculate the total head movement using: (a) FCFS (b) SSTF (c) SCAN (d) C-SCAN algorithms. Also explain why SSTF may cause starvation.
keyPoints: SCAN: 53в†’65в†’67в†’98в†’122в†’124в†’183в†’37в†’14 = 299 tracks (going to end then reverse)

questions.question: Explain the LOOK and C-LOOK disk scheduling algorithms. A disk has 200 tracks (0-199). The read-write head is currently at track 65 and the pending requests are at tracks: 12, 119, 45, 90, 78, 156, 31, 99. Calculate the seek time for LOOK algorithm assuming head moves towards higher track numbers first.
keyPoints: LOOK is similar to SCAN but doesn't go to the end of the disk, it reverses at the last request
C-LOOK is similar to C-SCAN but reverses at the last request, not at disk ends
LOOK order for given requests: 65в†’78в†’90в†’99в†’119в†’156в†’45в†’31 = 237 tracks
LOOK is more efficient than SCAN as it avoids unnecessary travel to disk ends
